data.title: Commodity Rally Led By Gold, Silver And TSX-V: Big Trade Now Or After A Summer Dip?
data.description:
The commodity rally, such as it has been so far, should take on a new intensity, possibly after a summer cool-down

Below I present for you one of those charts trying to say so much it could make your eyes water and your head spin. The chart is trying to quantify the history of loosely related markets (gold, silver, broad commodities and even the Canadian TSX-V index).

The reason we are doing this work is not to be bullish on the gold price, which we have been since the monetary metal took out the bull gateway (1378) in 2019. It is not to be bullish on silver, which is and has been bullish (in a lagging manner to gold) since its pattern breakout a year ago.

The reason we are doing this work is to gauge the coming of a commodity rally that goes beyond the wobbly upward biased pattern in the CRB index since June 2023. Thus far, we have earned profits in gold (which I personally am not booking on this long-term insurance policy), paper and realized profits in gold stocks and on certain denizens of the TSX-V, which has been rising since Q4, 2023.

But according to the CRB index and its rise to clear resistance, broader aspects of the party have not even begun yet. Gold, as it often does, has led the process. Silver has caught on and just recently started to bounce vs. gold, and the TSX-V has been rallying in opposition to the declining Silver/Gold ratio (but in positive correlation to CRB’s upward bias).

  • Gold led everything else, as it often does, by ending its bear market in 2019.
  • Silver took a final nose-dive in the COVID crash (silver is not gold, folks) and then shot upward, in alignment with the new, post-bailout “inflation trades” in CRB and TSX-V.
  • The precious metals then began a well-earned and extended corrective phase as the Silver/Gold ratio declined, as did the TSX-V. The CRB index, an index of cyclical commodities, continued upward as cyclical markets – including stocks – benefited from the inflation-fueled economic recovery.
  • With gold probably due to ease in relative performance to silver – and by extension, commodities – over the coming months/years, the question now becomes ‘will commodities break out imminently or after a summer cool down?’

Gold tends to lead a new inflation cycle, sometimes by a country mile. Silver is catching on. The CRB index is poised, and TSX-V is trending. What’s missing from the picture?

The Silver/Gold ratio (SGR) is bouncing, but still squarely in a downtrend. We have been watching for silver to bottom vs. gold and take up leadership since this (now public) update in January. However, short-term charts will direct whether a real break upward in the SGR will come imminently or after a summer cool-down/correction in many markets, including the precious metals and commodities.

It is important to keep in mind that the SGR is bouncing as expected, but within a daily chart downtrend. CRB is at resistance, and TSX-V has a resistance objective above at 800-850. We will manage these considerations on a weekly basis. But either way, history favors a tradable commodity rally near-term, or an even better one if a summer correction ensues first.

The above is not a complete analysis. It is a generalization of what is in play. We will manage more fine-tuned parameters each week to be sure we’re not caught guessing about the timing. I do not provide guru-like “instructions”. Only perma-promoters do that. We operate to plans based on logic and the facts of history, along with parameters to see those plans through.

data.title: Tracking John Rogers' Ariel Investments Portfolio - Q1 2025 Update
data.description:
This article is part of a series that provides an ongoing analysis of the changes made to Ariel Investments’ 13F stock portfolio on a quarterly basis. It is based on Ariel Investments’ regulatory 13F Form filed on 05/15/2025. John Rogers’ 13F portfolio value decreased from $9.37B to $8.51B this quarter. The portfolio is diversified with recent 13F reports showing around 150 positions. There are 35 securities that are significantly large (more than ~1% of the portfolio each) and they are the focus of this article. The largest five stakes are Mattel, Jones Lang LaSalle, Madison Square Garden Entertainment, Lazard, and First American Financial. They add up to ~15% of the portfolio. Please visit our Tracking John Rogers’ Ariel Investments Portfolio series to get an idea of their investment philosophy and our last update for the fund’s moves during Q4 2024.

Their flagship mutual fund is the Ariel Fund (MUTF:ARGFX) incepted in 1986. Ariel Fund has a lifetime annualized return of 10.17% compared to 10.29% for the Russell 2500 Index and 10.82% for the S&P 500 Index. The other mutual funds in the group are Ariel Appreciation Fund (MUTF:CAAPX), Ariel Focus Fund (MUTF:ARFFX), Ariel International Fund (MUTF:AINTX), and Ariel Global Fund (MUTF:AGLOX). They also co-manage the Schwab Ariel ESG ETF (NYSEARCA:SAEF).

Stake Disposals:

Royal Caribbean Cruises Ltd. (RCL): The 1.13% of the portfolio stake in RCL was disposed this quarter at prices between ~$194 and ~$277. The original position was built during the first three quarters of 2022 at prices between ~$31 and ~$91. The stock currently trades at ~$268. They harvested gains.

Stake Increases:

Madison Square Garden Ent (MSGE): MSGE is a large (top three) ~3% of the portfolio long-term stake. The position was increased by ~15% during the last quarter at prices between $33.05 and $43.75. The stock currently trades at $37.39. There was a minor ~2% increase this quarter.

Lazard Ltd. (LAZ): LAZ is a 2.86% of the portfolio position first purchased in 2009 at prices between ~$19 and ~$38. The next year saw a stake-tripling at prices between ~$25 and ~$36. In 2014 there was a ~25% selling at prices between ~$39 and ~$50. Recent activity follows. The three quarters through Q3 2021 saw a ~45% stake increase at prices between $38.70 and $48.75. The stock is now at $43.55. There was a ~40% trimming in the two years through Q2 2024. The last three quarters saw only minor adjustments.

Note: Ariel Investments has a ~5.4% ownership stake in Lazard.

J. M. Smucker (SJM): SJM is a 2.62% of the portfolio position established last year at prices between ~$106 and ~$135. The stock currently trades below that range at $95.96. There was a minor ~3% stake increase this quarter.

Envista Holdings (NVST): NVST is a 2.46% of the portfolio position. It was built during the last seven quarters at prices between ~$15 and ~$36. The stock currently trades at $18.61.

Sphere Entertainment (SPHR): The SPHR position came about as a result of the spin-out from MSG Entertainment. They had 6.66M shares of MSG Entertainment which now trades under this ticker. The net stake was reduced by ~20% during Q2 2023 and another ~15% in the next quarter. Q2 2024 saw a ~8% increase at prices between $32.49 and $49.16. That was followed by another similar increase during the last quarter at prices between $36.79 and $48.91. This quarter also saw a ~10% increase at prices between $16.06 and $22.68. The stock currently trades at $18.61.

Note: they control ~22% of the business.

Generac Holdings (GNRC): GNRC is a 2.40% stake that saw a ~27% increase during Q1 2023 at prices between ~$98 and ~$135. There was a ~15% increase during Q3 2023 at prices between ~$103 and ~$154. Q2 2024 saw a ~10% trimming at prices between ~$127 and ~$154. That was followed by a ~20% reduction during the last quarter at prices between $154.28 and $195.11. This quarter saw a ~10% stake increase at prices between ~$123 and ~$165. The stock is now at ~$126.

Mohawk Industries (MHK): MHK is a 2.34% of the portfolio position built over the three years through Q1 2022 at prices between ~$117 and ~$230. The stake was decreased by 13% during Q1 2024 at prices between ~$98 and ~$131. The last three quarters saw a ~10% trimming. The stock is now at ~$98. There was a minor ~3% stake increase this quarter.

Resideo Technologies (REZI): The ~2% REZI stake was built during the three quarters through Q2 2022 at prices between ~$19.50 and ~$28. There was a ~20% reduction during Q2 2024 at prices between $19.20 and $22.34. The stock currently trades at $20.96. The last two quarters also saw minor trimming. This quarter saw a ~5% stake increase.

Note: They have a ~7.3% ownership stake in the business.

Charles River Laboratories (CRL): The ~2% CRL stake saw a ~50% stake increase during Q2 2023 at prices between ~$184 and ~$210. The next quarter saw a minor increase while during Q4 2023 there was marginal trimming. The stake was decreased by 11% in the next quarter at prices between ~$204 and ~$273. There was a ~20% stake increase during the last quarter at prices between $178.58 and $220.69. The stock currently trades at ~$145. There was a minor ~3% stake increase this quarter.

Gentex Corp (GNTX): The 1.94% GNTX stake was purchased in Q3 2021 at prices between ~$27.50 and ~$38. Q3 2023 saw a ~18% selling at prices between ~$29 and ~$34. There was a ~11% stake increase this quarter at prices between ~$23 and ~$29. The stock currently trades at $21.39.

Bio-Rad Labs (BIO), BOK Financial (BOKF), Brinks Co (BCO), Core Laboratories (CLB), Interpublic Group (IPG), Manchester United (MANU), Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCLH), and OneSpaWorld Holdings (OSW): These positions were increased this quarter.

Note: They have significant ownership stakes in Manchester United, Core Laboratories, and OneSpaWorld Holdings.

Stake Decreases:

Mattel Inc. (MAT): MAT is currently at 3.16% of the portfolio. It was first purchased during 2016 at prices between ~$25 and ~$34. Next year saw a stake doubling at prices between ~$13 and ~$30.50. 2018 also saw a one-third stake increase at prices between ~$9.50 and ~$18. The stock currently trades at $18.97. The last several quarters saw only minor adjustments.

Jones Lang LaSalle (JLL): The ~3% JLL position is currently the largest 13F stake. It is a very long-term position first purchased in 2001. Next year saw a huge stake build-up at prices between ~$14.50 and ~$25. The position had seen selling since 2004. The bulk of the selling happened in 2006 at prices between ~$55 and ~$93. Recent activity follows. H2 2020 saw a ~25% stake increase at prices between ~$90 and ~$154. That was followed with a ~11% increase during Q1 2023 at prices between ~$137 and ~$185. There was a ~8% trimming during Q2 2024 at prices between ~$173 and ~$212. The next quarter also saw a ~12% reduction at prices between ~$199 and ~$272. There was similar selling during the last quarter at prices between $246.93 and $283.67. The stock currently trades at ~$237. This quarter saw a minor ~2% trimming.

First American Financial (FAF): FAF is a 2.85% of the portfolio stake established in 2011 at prices between ~$11 and ~$17. 2013 also saw a ~25% stake increase at prices between ~$21 and ~$28. Recent activity follows: There was a ~25% increase during Q1 2021 at prices between ~$51 and ~$58. The next quarter saw a ~15% selling at prices between ~$64 and ~$81. There was a ~10% stake increase during the last quarter at prices between $61.03 and $69.48. The stock is now at $57.78. There was a ~8% trimming this quarter.

Affiliated Managers Group (AMG): The 2.78% AMG stake saw a ~9% increase during Q2 2024 at prices between ~$148 and ~$170. The stock is now at ~$185. The last three quarters saw only minor adjustments.

ADT Inc. (ADT): ADT is currently at 2.59% of the portfolio. The original stake was increased by ~30% during Q1 2024 at prices between ~$6 and ~$7.50. The next quarter also saw a ~3% stake increase. The stock currently trades at $8.14. There was a ~12% trimming in the last three quarters.

Carlyle Group (CG): The 2.48% CG stake was built during the two quarters through Q3 2023 at prices between $27.50 and $42. The stake was decreased by 24% during Q1 2024 at prices between $37.83 and $47.23. The stock currently trades at $47.07. The last four quarters saw only minor adjustments.

Northern Trust (NTRS): NTRS is a 2.27% very long-term position first purchased in 2002. The 2002-2004 timeframe saw a ~10M share stake built at prices between ~$30 and ~$60. The position has since been sold down. The bulk of the selling was in the 2005-2007 timeframe at prices between ~$42 and ~$81. The last quarter saw a ~12% trimming at prices between ~$87 and ~$110. That was followed by another similar reduction this quarter at prices between ~$95 and ~$115. The stock currently trades at ~$111.

Kennametal Inc. (KMT): KMT is a ~2% of the portfolio position. It was established in 2014 at prices between $34 and $52. The position had seen minor buying over the years. Q1 2020 saw a ~15% stake increase at prices between $15 and $37. The three quarters through Q3 2021 had seen another ~43% stake increase at prices between ~$33 and ~$42. The stock currently trades at $22. The last few quarters have seen minor trimming.

Note: Ariel Investments has a ~10.3% ownership stake in Kennametal Inc.

Check Point Software (CHKP): The 1.81% stake in CHKP was built during H2 2023 at prices between ~$125 and ~$154. There was a ~30% trimming over the last four quarters. That was followed by a ~40% selling this quarter at prices between ~$179 and ~$234. The stock currently trades at ~$218.

Adtalem Global Education (ATGE), Axalta Coating (AXTA), Boyd Gaming (BYD), Laboratory Corp (LH), Madison Square Garden Sports (MSGS), Middleby (MIDD), Prestige Consumer Healthcare (PBH), and Paramount Global (PARA): These positions were reduced during the quarter.

Note: They have significant ownership stakes in Adtalem Global Education, Leslie’s (LESL), and Prestige Consumer Healthcare.
